# @zakkster/lite-headless/progress

Headless progress indicator. Linear bar or circular ring; determinate
or indeterminate. The primitive owns reactive value clamping + ARIA
painting + a CSS custom property `--progress` (0..1) on the root; the
consumer provides the markup and CSS.

## Quick example — linear

```js
import { createProgress } from "@zakkster/lite-headless/progress";

const pg = createProgress({
    value: 0,
    max: 100,
    label: "Uploading file",
    onChange: (v, f) => console.log(`${v} (${(f*100).toFixed(0)}%)`),
    onComplete: () => console.log("done"),
});

pg.attachRoot(rootEl);     // role=progressbar + --progress painted here
pg.attachBar(barEl);       // optional: bar element gets --progress + data-progress
```

```html
<div class="progress">
    <div data-progress-bar></div>
</div>
```

```css
.progress {
    height: 0.5rem;
    background: var(--bg-2);
    border-radius: 999px;
    overflow: hidden;
}
[data-progress-bar] {
    height: 100%;
    background: var(--primary);
    transform-origin: left center;
    transform: scaleX(var(--progress));
    transition: transform 200ms;
}
```

## Quick example — circular

```js
const pg = createProgress({
    value: 0,
    max: 100,
    variant: "circular",
    label: "Saving",
});
pg.attachRoot(rootEl);
pg.attachIndicator(indEl);
```

```html
<div class="progress-ring">
    <svg viewBox="0 0 36 36">
        <circle class="track" cx="18" cy="18" r="16" />
        <circle class="ind" data-progress-indicator cx="18" cy="18" r="16" />
    </svg>
</div>
```

```css
.progress-ring svg {
    width: 3rem;
    height: 3rem;
    transform: rotate(-90deg);   /* start at 12 o'clock */
}
.progress-ring circle {
    fill: none;
    stroke-width: 3;
}
.progress-ring .track { stroke: var(--bg-3); }
.progress-ring .ind {
    stroke: var(--primary);
    stroke-linecap: round;
    /* circumference = 2π * r = 2π * 16 ≈ 100.53 */
    stroke-dasharray: 100.53;
    stroke-dashoffset: calc(100.53 * (1 - var(--progress)));
    transition: stroke-dashoffset 200ms;
}
```

## Options

| Option           | Default     | Notes                                          |
| ---------------- | ----------- | ---------------------------------------------- |
| `value`          | `0`         | Current value (clamped to [min, max])          |
| `min`            | `0`         | Lower bound                                    |
| `max`            | `100`       | Upper bound                                    |
| `variant`        | `"linear"`  | `"linear"` or `"circular"` (controls data-variant only) |
| `indeterminate`  | `false`     | Hides aria-valuenow, sets data-indeterminate   |
| `label`          | -           | aria-label on root (skipped if root already has aria-label/labelledby) |
| `valueText`      | -           | Custom aria-valuetext (overrides auto "NN%")   |
| `onChange`       | -           | `(value, fraction) => void` fires on every paint |
| `onComplete`     | -           | Fires once on first hit of max; refires if value drops then returns to max |

## ARIA

| Element      | Attributes painted by primitive                            |
| ------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| Root         | `role="progressbar"` · `aria-valuenow` (omitted when indeterminate) · `aria-valuemin` · `aria-valuemax` · `aria-valuetext` · `aria-label` (or `aria-labelledby` via attachLabel) · `data-progress-root` · `data-complete` (when value === max) · `data-loading` (when value < max) · `data-variant` · `data-indeterminate` |
| Bar          | `data-progress-bar` · `data-progress="NN"` (0-100 int)     |
| Indicator    | `data-progress-indicator` · `data-progress="NN"`           |

The CSS custom property `--progress` (0..1 number) is set on root, bar,
and indicator. Consumer CSS uses it for whatever rendering they choose:
width %, scaleX, stroke-dashoffset, conic-gradient angle, etc.

## State machine

```
construct
    │
    ▼
┌──────────┐   value < max     ┌──────────┐
│ loading  │ ────────────────► │ loading  │  (paints repeat as value changes)
└──────────┘                   └────┬─────┘
                                    │
                              value >= max
                                    │
                                    ▼
                              ┌──────────┐
                              │ complete │  data-complete
                              └──────────┘  onComplete fires once
```

`data-complete` is set whenever `value >= max && !indeterminate`,
otherwise `"loading"`. `onComplete` fires on the 0→1 transition; if
`setValue` later drops below `max` and returns to `max`, it fires again.

## Indeterminate mode

```js
pg.setIndeterminate(true);    // hides aria-valuenow, sets data-indeterminate=""
```

When indeterminate:
- `aria-valuenow` is removed from root (per ARIA spec for indeterminate progressbar)
- `data-indeterminate` is set (consumer CSS animates the bar/indicator)
- `aria-valuetext` defaults to "Loading" (override with `valueText` / `setValueText`)
- `data-loading` stays set regardless of value

Consumer CSS handles the animation:

```css
[data-progress-root][data-indeterminate] [data-progress-bar] {
    animation: indeterminate-sweep 1.5s ease-in-out infinite;
}
@keyframes indeterminate-sweep {
    0%   { transform: translateX(-100%); }
    100% { transform: translateX(100%); }
}
```

## Bounds + clamping

- `value` is always clamped into `[min, max]` on `setValue` and on construction
- Changing `min`/`max` re-clamps the current value
- `fraction()` returns `(value - min) / (max - min)`, clamped to `[0, 1]`
- When `max <= min`, `fraction()` returns 0 (treat as "no progress")
- Non-finite inputs (`NaN`, `Infinity`, non-numbers) to `setValue`/`setMin`/`setMax`
  are silently ignored (no exception, no state change)

## Element wrapper

```html
<lite-progress value="42" max="100" label="Uploading">
    <div data-progress-bar></div>
</lite-progress>

<lite-progress value="0" max="100" indeterminate label="Saving">
    <div data-progress-bar></div>
</lite-progress>

<lite-progress value="72" max="100" variant="circular" label="Sync">
    <svg viewBox="0 0 36 36">
        <circle class="track" cx="18" cy="18" r="16" fill="none"/>
        <circle data-progress-indicator cx="18" cy="18" r="16" fill="none"/>
    </svg>
</lite-progress>
```

### Reactive attributes

| Attribute        | Effect                                          |
| ---------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| `value`          | Reactive: changing it calls setValue            |
| `min` / `max`    | Reactive: changing recalculates fraction        |
| `indeterminate`  | Flag (presence/absence)                         |
| `value-text`     | Custom aria-valuetext                           |
| `label`          | Static aria-label (read once on attach)         |
| `variant`        | Static `"linear"` or `"circular"` (read once)   |

### Imperative API + events

```js
host.setValue(50)
host.setMin(0)
host.setMax(200)
host.setIndeterminate(true)
host.setValueText("step 3 of 7")
host.value         // current value
host.fraction      // 0..1
host.isComplete    // boolean

host.addEventListener("change",   e => /* { value, fraction } */);
host.addEventListener("complete", e => /* {} */);
```

## Common patterns

### File upload progress

```js
const pg = createProgress({ value: 0, max: file.size, label: file.name });
pg.attachRoot(rootEl);
pg.attachBar(barEl);

xhr.upload.addEventListener("progress", e => {
    if (e.lengthComputable) pg.setValue(e.loaded);
});
xhr.addEventListener("load", () => pg.setValue(file.size));
```

### Multi-step wizard

```js
const pg = createProgress({ value: 1, max: 5, valueText: "Step 1 of 5" });

function goToStep(n) {
    pg.setValue(n);
    pg.setValueText(`Step ${n} of 5`);
}
```

### Indeterminate while waiting, determinate once known

```js
const pg = createProgress({ indeterminate: true, label: "Searching..." });
pg.attachRoot(rootEl);

api.estimateSize().then(total => {
    pg.setMax(total);
    pg.setIndeterminate(false);   // switch to determinate now
    api.stream().on("byte", () => pg.setValue(v => v + 1));
});
```

## When NOT to use

- **Indeterminate spinners with no progress context** — a CSS-only spinner
  is lighter. Use this primitive only when there's actual progress to
  communicate (even "we don't know how much" is meaningful when paired
  with `indeterminate: true`).
- **Stepper / wizard UI with named steps** — use `stepper` instead, which
  has named-step ARIA and keyboard navigation. `progress` is for a single
  number; `stepper` is for a sequence of labeled stations.
- **Loading skeletons** — those should use a dedicated skeleton primitive
  (coming) which coordinates multiple placeholders revealing together.

## CSS contract

This primitive's painted attributes follow the canonical CSS contract
in `docs/CSS_CONTRACT.md`. See that doc for the cross-primitive
taxonomy (booleans like `data-open` / `data-disabled` / `data-loading`,
enums like `data-status` / `data-side`, ARIA pairings, host accessor
naming, and event naming).
